title: Fix iframe to fill full screen on mobile

requirements.summary: Make the embedded iframe fill the entire mobile viewport with no gaps or scrollbars, while keeping the branded bar visible as an overlay
acceptanceCriteria: On a mobile device (iOS and Android), the iframe fills the entire screen edge-to-edge with no visible gaps or blank areas.
The html, body, and all wrapper/container elements have no default margin, padding, or height restrictions that would constrain the iframe.
The iframe uses position: fixed and covers 100vw × 100dvh (or 100vh fallback), starting from inset: 0.
The branded bar is overlaid on top of the iframe without reducing the iframe's height.
No horizontal or vertical scrollbars appear outside the iframe on the host page.
The layout works correctly on iPhone (with notch/dynamic island) and Android devices.

file_operations.description: Reset html and body to fill the entire viewport with no margin, padding, or overflow. Apply position: fixed, width: 100vw, height: 100vh with 100dvh fallback, inset: 0, and overflow: hidden to ensure the page container fills the full screen on mobile devices.

Restructure the App component layout to make the iframe fill the entire viewport using position: fixed, inset: 0, width: 100vw, and height: 100dvh (with 100vh fallback). Position the branded bar (containing the banner frontend/public/assets/generated/revelation-walls-banner.dim_1200x120.png) absolutely or fixed on top of the iframe as an overlay without reducing the iframe's height. Remove any wrapper containers or CSS that constrain the iframe's dimensions. Ensure no scrollbars appear on the host page.
